{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why:

- It is the edge that creates both reported problems: the 3-file cycle and the layer violation.
- `domain` depending on `presentation` is the wrong direction architecturally. Domain code should not know how invoices are rendered.
- Breaking another edge, such as `application -> domain` or `presentation -> application/domain`, would likely damage the intended layering more.

The cycle is:

```text
src/application/billing-flow.ts
 -> src/domain/billing.ts
 -> src/presentation/invoice-view.ts
 -> src/application/billing-flow.ts
```

Best fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral lower-level module, or invert it behind a domain-owned interface/type. If the dependency is only for TypeScript types, convert it to `import type`; otherwise extract a shared type/helper into something like:

```text
src/domain/invoice-types.ts
```

or

```text
src/shared/invoice-formatting.ts
```

depending on whether it is pure domain data or presentation formatting."}}
